meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no design skills (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no design skills. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master Client Workflow: Overcome Design Gaps (Advanced Guide)
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between code and design. Learn a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to build high-end apps without design skills.
content.answer_block: Stop letting design paralysis stall your development. By implementing a standardized client workflow, you decouple aesthetic decisions from logic. Use Antigravity for rapid UI scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, turning abstract requirements into high-fidelity, functional interfaces without needing a background in graphic design.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks perfecting your backend logic, only to hit the 'design wall' where your brilliant code meets a lackluster interface. This is the silent killer of student projects—the moment where technical excellence is overshadowed by a lack of visual polish. But here is the secret: professional software isn't built by artists; it's built by systems.

By adopting a rigorous client-facing workflow, you shift from being a 'coder trying to design' to a 'product engineer delivering value.' We will utilize Antigravity to handle the structural heavy lifting and Claude Code to act as your design-system architect, ensuring your portfolio pieces look like they were shipped by a senior-led team.

steps.title: Define the Design System Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, establish a strict design token schema (colors, spacing, typography) to prevent scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a design system token schema in JSON for a modern, clean SaaS dashboard. Include primary/secondary colors, 8px grid spacing, and font scale.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate tailwind.config.js based on the provided design token schema'

steps.title: Wireframe the Component Architecture
steps.description: Map out the user journey into atomic components to ensure your workflow remains modular and scalable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a component architecture map for a user dashboard, breaking down the sidebar, data grid, and action modals into atomic units.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create React component stubs based on the architecture map, using functional patterns'

steps.title: Automate UI Scaffolding
steps.description: Use your generated schema to spin up the UI shell, ensuring consistency across every page automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the base layout shell using the defined tokens. Ensure mobile responsiveness and dark mode support.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the layout shell in the project, ensuring all layout components are responsive'

steps.title: Implement Intelligent Feedback Loops
steps.description: Create a client-review loop where Claude iterates on UI based on specific UX constraints rather than subjective preferences.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a checklist for evaluating UI components against accessibility standards and usability heuristics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Audit the current UI components against the accessibility checklist and suggest high-impact improvements'

steps.title: Finalize and Polish
steps.description: Perform a final pass to unify spacing and contrast, ensuring the final output looks professional and polished.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a final styling pass to ensure consistency in whitespace, border-radius, and shadow depth across all components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the final styling pass to all components to ensure visual harmony'

pro_tips: Always define your design tokens in a single source of truth file before touching CSS.
Use Claude Code to enforce strict naming conventions to prevent 'class-name soup'.
Focus on whitespace and alignment; 90% of 'bad design' is just inconsistent margin and padding.
Treat your UI components as APIs; if a component is hard to use, it's poorly designed.
Iterate on your design system as you build; don't try to build the perfect UI on day one.
content.next_steps: Now that you have a repeatable workflow, apply it to a new feature branch in your current project. Use the feedback loops to refine your UI, and then deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify to see your system in action.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to build professional-grade interfaces that land jobs and impress mentors. Stop struggling with design and start engineering it. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
